features.summary: Apple reported record Q3 FY2021 results with revenue of $81.4 billion, up 36% year-over-year, driven by strong double-digit growth across all product categories and geographic segments. iPhone revenue hit a June quarter record of $39.6 billion (up 50%), while Services reached an all-time record of $17.5 billion (up 33%). The company returned $29 billion to shareholders and maintained strong customer satisfaction scores across products, with the iPhone 12 family continuing to see exceptional demand globally.
features.management_outlook: Management expects very strong double-digit revenue growth in Q4 but below the 36% rate seen in Q3 due to three factors: less favorable foreign exchange impact, services growth normalizing from elevated levels, and greater supply constraints primarily affecting iPhone and iPad. Gross margins are guided to 41.5%-42.5% range. The company remains optimistic about long-term growth drivers including early 5G adoption, growing installed base reaching new records, and continued innovation in products and services.
features.risks: Supply constraints are expected to worsen in Q4, primarily impacting iPhone and iPad production due to industry-wide semiconductor shortages and legacy node constraints. COVID-related uncertainties persist with the Delta variant creating uneven global recovery patterns. The company faces higher freight costs and potential regulatory pressures in key markets like China, though component costs continue to decline in aggregate.
features.key_drivers: Strong customer loyalty with 97% satisfaction for iPhone 12 family and growing active device installed base across all segments drove performance. Services growth was supported by over 700 million paid subscriptions (up 150 million year-over-year) and strong performance in advertising and Apple Care as markets reopened. The M1 chip transition powered record Mac and iPad performance, while emerging markets showed exceptional strength with double-digit growth across multiple regions including India, Latin America, and Vietnam.
